title: Ian Goodfellow - Research Scientist @ Google DeepMind
excerpts:
Ian Goodfellow - Research Scientist @ Google DeepMind
Ian Goodfellow is a Research Scientist at DeepMind. He was a Director at Apple. He attended the Université de Montréal.

title: Ian Goodfellow
excerpts:
Section Title: Ian Goodfellow
Content:
**Short Bio**
Ian Goodfellow is a Principal Scientist at Google DeepMind. He first became involved in AI research at the Stanford AI Lab as an undergraduate working with Andrew Ng. He was a relatively early advocate of deep learning and completed a PhD in machine learning at Université de Montréal, and wrote the MIT Press textbook Deep Learning with his PhD co-advisors, Yoshua Bengio and Aaron Courville. His invention of Generative Adversarial Networks in 2014 helped to popularize generative models. As a research scientist at Google and OpenAI, he has contributed to machine learning security and privacy and been an advocate for responsible AI development and diversity and inclusion in the AI industry. He has also served as Apple Special Project Group's Director of Machine Learning.
**Title**
Generative AI: Past, Present, and Future

title: Top 10+ Machine Learning Keynote Speakers
excerpts:
Section Title: Mark Matthews > Who Are the Best Machine Learning Keynote Speakers? > 3. Ian Goodfellow
Content:
[](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Z6rxFNMGdn0)
Ian Goodfellow is a leading AI and machine learning expert, renowned for inventing Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs). A former Director of Machine Learning at Apple and Research Scientist at DeepMind, Ian has contributed significantly to deep learning and AI. He has worked with top organisations like Google and OpenAI, authored the "Deep Learning" textbook, and been recognised among MIT's 35 Innovators Under 35. Ian's keynote speeches provide pioneering insights into AI, generative models, and machine learning advancements.

title: Ian Goodfellow - Wikipedia
excerpts: **Ian J. Goodfellow** (born 1987 <sup>[[ 1 ]]()</sup> ) is an American computer scientist , engineer , and executive , most noted for his work on artificial neural networks and deep learning . He is a research scientist at Google DeepMind , <sup>[[ 2 ]]()</sup> was previously employed as a research scientist at Google Brain and director of machine learning at Apple as well as one of the first employees at OpenAI , and has made several important contributions to the field of deep learning , including the invention of the generative adversarial network (GAN). Goodfellow co-wrote, as the first author, the textbook *Deep Learning* (2016) <sup>[[ 3 ]]()</sup> and wrote the chapter on deep learning in the authoritative textbook of the field of artificial intelligence, *Artificial Intelligence: A Modern Approach* <sup>[[ 4 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 5 ]]()</sup> (used in more than 1,500 universities in 135 countries). <sup>[[ 6 ]]()</sup>

title: Ian Goodfellow on Inventing GANs - Editorial - Le Random
excerpts:
Section Title: **Ian Goodfellow on Inventing GANs**
Content:
*In this written form of the conversation, deep learning engineer and generative adversarial network (GAN) inventor Ian Goodfellow speaks with Peter Bauman (* [*Monk Antony*](https://x.com/monkantony_tez) *) in depth about the legendary origins of GANs, their unexpected success and indelible impact on both twenty-first-century image making and AI research. The full audio form of the conversation is now available* [*on*](https://open.spotify.com/episode/116hKZempQsL20gnoWMCS6) [*podcast*](https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podcast/31-ian-goodfellow-on-inventing-gans/id1726153340?i=1000735635188) *and* [*YouTube*](https://youtu.be/p-pBWXLlvfI) *.* ‍ **
Peter Bauman: GANs have played an outsized, enormously significant role in the development of deep generative models, especially in the transition from tasks like feature learning to actual synthesis and generation.** ‍
‍ **In your own words, can you explain what GANs are and what the
